eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Anthropic at 69.3% implied probability for the best AI model by end of June 2026, propelled by Claude Sonnet 4.6 and Opus 4.6 sweeping the top four spots in late April agent benchmarks on LMSYS Chatbot Arena derivatives, showcasing superior cooperation scores and low debate rates from refined RLHF techniques. Google's Gemini 3.1 Pro holds 22.5% as a close second, bolstered by record agent leaderboard performances earlier in Q2, maintaining balanced capabilities across reasoning and reliability. OpenAI's 6% reflects GPT-5.4's mid-pack #8 ranking and persistent rollout delays, while xAI's Grok 4.1 trails at 2.3% despite compute advantages. Key catalysts ahead include potential Claude 5 previews and Gemini updates at developer conferences, amid tight Elo races within 50 points among top labs.

markets.question: Exact Score: Villarreal CF 2 - 0 Levante UD?
markets.description: In the upcoming La Liga game between Villarreal CF and Levante UD, scheduled for May 2, 2026 at 8:00 AM ET: This market will resolve based on the final score of the Villarreal CF vs. Levante UD match originally scheduled for May 2, 2026 at 8:00 AM ET, considering only the result at the end of 90 minutes of regulation plus stoppage time; extra time and penalty shoot-outs are excluded. If the actual score is not one of the explicitly listed outcomes, the market resolves to "Any Other Score." If the match is postponed, the market will remain open until it is completed; if it is canceled with no make-up game, the market resolves to "0-0." The primary resolution source for this market will be the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if final official statistics are not published within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

This balance of Villarreal's attacking threat against Levante's counter potential keeps exact score probabilities tightly clustered around 17%, underscoring a closely contested matchup prone to narrow wins, draws, or surprises.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Buenos Aires be 18°C on May 3?
markets.description:

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Minister Pistarini Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/ar/ezeiza/SAEZ.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

Climatological May maxima average 19°C, reinforcing the positioning, though minor risks from increased cloud cover or sea breezes could trim peaks to 17°C. Updated model runs over the past 24 hours drove recent odds tightening; watch Servicio Meteorológico Nacional bulletins and intraday observations for refinements.

markets.question: Will Republicanos (REPUBLICANOS) win the most seats in the next Brazilian Senate election?
markets.description:
The next federal Senate election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026, in which two-thirds of the Senate’s 81 seats will be contested.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Brazilian Senate election. 

Only seats contested in the next Brazilian Senate election will be considered.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket strongly favors the Partido Liberal (PL) at 79.5% implied probability to win the most seats among the 27 up for renewal in Brazil's October 4, 2026 general election—one senator per state and the Federal District—driven by recent state-level polls from Quaest and Paraná Pesquisas showing PL candidates leading in multiple battlegrounds like Paraná and Tocantins, bolstered by high-profile recruits such as Sergio Moro. Governors from PL-aligned blocs renounced executive posts by early April deadlines to contest, strengthening slates amid projections from Congresso em Foco highlighting PL's dominance in majoritarian races. União Brasil trails at 9.5% with centro-direita momentum, NOVO at 7.1% via libertarian appeal, while PT at 2.7% reflects Lula's tepid approval and thinner benches; party conventions this summer could adjust dynamics.

markets.question: Will China GDP growth in Q2 2026 be between 4.0% and 4.3%?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to China's Y/Y Growth Rate (%) of Gross Domestic Product (GDP) in the "Preliminary Accounting Results of GDP" release for Q2 of 2026, scheduled for July 16, 2026.

The GDP release will be made available here: https://www.stats.gov.cn/english/PressRelease/

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If no data for the specified quarter is released by the date the next quarter's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available quarter.

Note: data from the initial release of the referenced GDP report is what will be used to resolve this market. Data may be revised during the following quarter or as a part of the next estimate's publication, however any revisions to GDP report data made after the initial release will not be considered for this market's resolution.

For the full release schedule, see: https://www.stats.gov.cn/english/PressRelease/ReleaseCalendar/202512/t20251226_1962154.html

markets.question: Will both candidates advancing to the general election for Governor of California from the June 2, 2026 primary be affiliated with the Republican Party?
markets.description:
The non-partisan primary election for Governor of California is scheduled to take place on June 2, 2026. The top two candidates in this election by number of votes won will advance to the general election for Governor of California.

This market will resolve according to the party affiliation of the listed candidates who advance from the primary to the general election for Governor of California. A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with a party.

In case any combination of parties advances that is not listed here, including all scenarios where an independent candidate advances, this market will resolve to "Other".

If no 2026 California gubernatorial primary takes place by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the results of the primary election for Governor of California as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the government of California, specifically the Office of the Secretary of State.

President Trump's endorsement of Hilton further solidified GOP cohesion. High undecideds and low turnout concerns persist ahead of the May 5 CNN debate and June 2 primary.

eventMetadata.context_description: Florida Governor Ron DeSantis, term-limited out of office in January 2027, faces slim odds of joining the Trump administration by June 30 amid unconfirmed rumors rather than official actions. However, no nomination, resignation announcement, or formal discussions have materialized in the past week, with DeSantis remaining focused on state duties. Traders' strong 86% No consensus reflects the tight two-month window, historical patterns of early-term cabinet fillings, and absence of momentum for a mid-governorship exit.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Chuck Schumer and John Thune nearly even as next Senate Majority Leader, reflecting a projected tossup for chamber control after the November 2026 midterms, where Republicans defend their current 53-47 majority amid 35 contested seats including specials in Florida and Ohio. Janet Mills, abruptly exited the race on April 30, boosting GOP incumbent Susan Collins' hold odds and narrowing Democratic paths to 51 seats. Midterm dynamics historically disadvantage the president's party, but GOP advantages in vulnerable Democratic seats keep it tight; primary outcomes, such as Maine's June 9 contest, and shifts in swing-state polls could widen the gap. Lower-tier contenders like Brian Schatz signal potential intraparty leadership challenges.

This market will resolve as soon as the listed value is reached, or once data has been released for the final week ending on or before June 5, 2026, and the listed value has not been reached.

markets.question: Will Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) hit (HIGH) $7.00 Week of May 4 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during the week of May 4 2026, any 1-minute candle for Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.OPEN%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.OPEN%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

eventMetadata.context_description: Early-season dominance by a deep crop of AL starters has kept the 2026 Cy Young race bunched, with Parker Messick's 21.9% implied probability edging Tarik Skubal's 21.5% amid sub-2.00 ERAs across the leaderboard. Messick, Cleveland Guardians rookie, earned the Pitch Hand Award for April after a 3-0 mark, 1.73 ERA, 0.88 WHIP, and 25.3% strikeouts over 36.1 innings, highlighted by an eight-inning gem versus Baltimore and a near no-hitter. Skubal leads MLB.com's first Cy Young poll with a 1.97 ERA and 38 strikeouts in 36.1 frames for Detroit, though his 2.72 mark in some splits tempers enthusiasm. Contenders like Yankees' Cam Schlittler (1.51 ERA, 0.74 WHIP), Jose Soriano (0.24 ERA, 5-0), Dylan Cease (1.82 ERA with Toronto), and Bryce Miller sustain pressure through elite peripherals, but small-sample volatility, innings durability, and second-half health will define separation in this wide-open rotation battle.

markets.question: Iran closes its airspace by May 8?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Iran initiates a major closure of its airspace, that is not solely due to weather conditions, by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A “major closure” is defined as a broad closure, cancellation, or suspension of commercial flights transiting, arriving in, and departing from Iranian airspace or a major Iranian Airspace region. A qualifying closure must apply generally to flights across Iran or a major Iranian airspace region. Limited cancellations, delays, or other partial closures will not qualify. Limited exceptions to a broad closure, however, will not disqualify such a closure from counting (e.g. exceptions for certain flights pre-approved by the Iranian Civil Aviation Authority may be permitted). 

Any non-weather complete suspension of commercial arrivals and departures affecting at least two of the following airports will qualify: Imam Khomeini International Airport (IKA), Mehrabad Airport (THR), Mashhad International Airport (MHD), Shiraz International Airport (SYZ), or Isfahan International Airport (IFN).

Qualifying previous examples include the January 2026 total closure of Iranian airspace except to international flights with express governmental permission (https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/iran-shuts-airspace-most-flights-flightradar24-says-2026-01-14/) and the April 2024 closure of Iran’s western airspace (https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/iranian-airports-cancel-flights-until-monday-morning-2024-04-14/). Non-qualifying examples include the January 27, 2026 partial closure of Iranian airspace around the Strait of Hormuz (https://www.aa.com.tr/en/middle-east/iran-issues-airspace-notice-for-military-drills-near-strait-of-hormuz/3812508) and the January 25 - April 25, 2026 VFR (Visual Flight Rules) flights suspension which did not broadly close commercial aviation in a qualifying region (https://www.intellinews.com/iran-suspends-general-aviation-and-vfr-flights-in-new-airspace-directive-421997/).

Warnings, No-Fly-Zones, or other flight restrictions imposed by airlines or countries other than Iran will not be sufficient for a “Yes” resolution.

Airspace closures which occur solely due to weather conditions will not qualify.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from Iranian aviation authorities and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Iran's airspace, closed nationwide since February 28 following large-scale U.S. and Israeli strikes on military targets, has undergone phased reopenings over the past two weeks, with eastern Tehran FIR access restored for overflights above FL285, domestic flights restarting April 22, and commercial operations from Tehran resuming May 1 amid cautious normalization. Western and central sectors remain restricted per ongoing NOTAMs, reflecting persistent post-conflict risks including IRGC drone interceptions and warnings against foreign incursions. Trader sentiment hinges on ceasefire durability, with potential escalations from airstrikes, proxy conflicts, or diplomatic breakdowns capable of triggering full re-closure; monitor Civil Aviation Authority updates and regional military maneuvers for shifts.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the IL-09 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the IL-09 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

Jan Schakowsky's retirement. Biss enters the November 3 general election with strong fundraising—over $250,000 banked in early April—and endorsements, contrasting weak Republican nominee John Elleson, a pastor with limited resources in a district last won by a GOP candidate decades ago. Trader consensus prices a Democratic victory at 92.5%, reflecting historical margins and low GOP turnout. Upsets remain possible via a major Biss scandal, national Republican midterm wave, or voting irregularities, though barriers are high.

markets.description:

This market will resolve to "Cultural y Deportiva Leonesa" if Cultural y Deportiva Leonesa win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Albacete Balompié".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on laliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Albacete Balompié vs. Cultural y Deportiva Leonesa - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the La Liga 2 game, scheduled for May 9 at 10:15 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Albacete Balompié hosts relegation-battling Cultural y Deportiva Leonesa in LaLiga 2 on May 9 at Estadio Carlos Belmonte, with trader consensus likely favoring the mid-table hosts due to home advantage and Leonesa's dismal form. Leonesa sit 22nd after recent defeats, including 2-1 at Mirandés on April 26, 1-2 home loss to Córdoba on April 18, and 1-0 at Granada on April 12, leaving them desperate for points in a must-win survival push amid their 8-8-20 record. Albacete, 13th in the table, lost 0-3 at Eibar on April 24 but won 4-1 versus Granada prior, showing attacking threat at home. Head-to-head history tilts toward Leonesa with two prior wins and three draws, though low-scoring (0.8 goals average); no major injuries reported, but long-term absences like Albacete's Antonio Puertas persist. Late-season stakes heighten upset potential for the visitors.

markets.question: Will an Iranian agent be charged in the US by May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any Federal or State jurisdiction of the United States formally charges or announces a criminal indictment of any individual alleged to be acting as an agent of the Iranian government between market creation and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

“Iranian agent” refers to any individual alleged to be acting on behalf of, under the direction of, or in coordination with the Iranian government (including its military or intelligence services) to conduct espionage, collect intelligence, or perform other covert operations that benefit Iran.

For the purposes of this market, the District of Columbia and any county, municipality, or other subdivision of a State shall be included within the definition of a State.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources; however, a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors "No" at 77.5% implied probability for an Iranian agent—such as an IRGC operative or spy engaged in espionage or assassination plots—being charged by the Department of Justice by May 31, reflecting the absence of qualifying DOJ or FBI indictments in recent weeks. The March conviction of Pakistani national Asif Merchant in an Iran-linked murder-for-hire scheme targeting Donald Trump marked the last major such case, but no new charges against Iranian intelligence personnel have followed. Recent actions, including the April 19 arrest of Iranian national Shamim Mafi at LAX for sanctions violations tied to brokering arms sales to Sudan and the April 25 indictment of Jafar Tafakori for migrant smuggling, involve economic crimes rather than explicit agency allegations. Amid heightened U.S. sanctions on Iran weapons networks and ongoing regional tensions, including a naval blockade, traders see insufficient momentum for a qualifying development before the deadline.

markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab for "Coding" is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Coding" Leaderboard tab at https://arena.ai/leaderboard/text/coding-no-style-control with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie still remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

markets.question: Abstract FDV above $200M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Abstract's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Abstract doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Spread: Club Independiente Petrolero (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Copa Sudamericana game, scheduled for April 15 at 6:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Club Independiente Petrolero" if Club Independiente Petrolero win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Caracas FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on conmebol.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Will François Ruffin win the 2027 French presidential election?
markets.description:
The next French presidential election is currently expected to be held around April 2027.  This market pertains to the outcome of the next French presidential election, regardless of whether it follows the scheduled end of the current term or is held earlier. 

The President of France is elected via a two-round system; a candidate must secure over 50% of the vote to win outright in the first round. If no candidate achieves this, the top two contenders advance to a runoff.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If, for any reason, the results of the election are not known by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the French Government, specifically the Ministry of the Interior (https://www.interieur.gouv.fr/).

Horizons leader Édouard Philippe, buoyed by his March 2026 Le Havre mayoral reelection and municipal gains for centrists, trails closely at 19.5% while polling strongest against Bardella in runoffs, per BFMTV and Verian surveys in late March-April. The race remains tight due to a crowded field splintering votes—Les Républicains endorsed Bruno Retailleau on April 20, leftists like Jean-Luc Mélenchon lag, and no candidate exceeds 50% in head-to-heads. Separation could emerge from center-right consolidation, internal party primaries, government crises, or scandals before the first round.

markets.question: Will Girona qualify for the League Phase of the 2026-27 UEFA Champions League?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team clinches a league phase spot in the 2026-27 Champions League per UEFA rules. Otherwise, the associated market will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to clinch a league phase spot in the 2026-27 Champions League (e.g. they cannot mathematically achieve a Champions League place, cannot qualify through play in European or cup competitions, etc.), the associated market will resolve to "No". 

If the 2025-26 LaLiga season is cancelled, postponed after August 31, 2026 11:59 PM ET, or the teams that qualified for the 2026-27 Champions League have not been officially confirmed by UEFA within that timeframe, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from UEFA and/or LaLiga; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Latvia's Atvara and "Ēnā" (22.1%) struggles with niche folk appeal and zero jury points in previews, while Israel's Noam Bettan "Michelle" (21.3%) faces boycott calls from over 1,000 artists and expected televote backlash amid geopolitical tensions. Rehearsals starting this week could spark momentum shifts via staging reveals and live buzz.

markets.question: LoL: FURIA Esports vs RED Canids - Game 1 Winner
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between FURIA Esports and RED Canids in the CBLOL Regular Season, initially scheduled for May 2 at 3:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "FURIA Esports" if FURIA Esports win Game 1 against RED Canids.

This market will resolve to "RED Canids" if RED Canids win Game 1 against FURIA Esports.

If the match begins but is not completed, and Game 1 is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on the completed Game 1. 

If Game 1 is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. 

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: LoL: FURIA Esports vs RED Canids (BO3) - CBLOL Regular Season
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between FURIA Esports and RED Canids in the CBLOL Regular Season, initially scheduled for May 2 at 3:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "FURIA Esports" if FURIA Esports win the match against RED Canids.

This market will resolve to "RED Canids" if RED Canids win the match against FURIA Esports.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: RED Canids enters their CBLOL Split 1 regular season BO3 against FURIA undefeated at 6-0 series record with a dominant 12-1 map tally, fueling trader consensus on their top-standing momentum after a 2-0 sweep of LØS last weekend. FURIA sits tied for second at 4-2 following a gritty 2-1 comeback win over LOUD on April 26 after dropping a clean 0-2 to Fluxo W7M, showcasing resilience but vulnerability in macro execution. Head-to-head favors RED, who dispatched FURIA 3-1 in the recent CBLOL Cup; stable rosters and current patch meta emphasize mid-lane duels like RED's Kaze, with playoff seeding implications heightening stakes in this Week 6 clash.

eventMetadata.context_description: Michael Minogue's dominant trader consensus at 84% in the Massachusetts Republican gubernatorial primary reflects his landslide endorsement at the April 2026 MassGOP state convention, where he secured over 70% of delegate votes and easily met the 15% ballot qualification threshold. Brian Shortsleeve advanced with a narrow 15.5% to force a September 1 primary head-to-head, positioning him at 12%, while former Housing Secretary Mike Kennealy fell short and suspended his campaign, dropping to 1.9%. Minogue's momentum as a wealthy biotech executive and GOP donor has solidified party support amid earlier polls showing a fragmented field, with the convention narrowing the contest and boosting his path to the nomination against Democratic incumbent Maura Healey.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following Rep. Jerry Nadler's retirement from New York's 12th Congressional District after over three decades, the June 23 closed Democratic primary remains tightly contested, with trader consensus pricing Assemblyman Micah Lasher at 39.5% implied probability ahead of Alex Bores at 34% and Jack Schlossberg at 17.5% in a fragmented 18-candidate field. Gov. Kathy Hochul's April 13 endorsement of her former policy director Lasher bolstered his establishment support, while Bores' mid-April internal poll showed Schlossberg leading narrowly at 22% to Bores' 19% and Lasher's 14% amid 28% undecideds. Recent 92nd Street Y forums emphasized differences on Trump accountability and local priorities, but high fragmentation and lack of neutral public polls keep the race fluid; upcoming FEC Q1 fundraising reports, union endorsements, or independent surveys could create separation.

markets.question: Will NVIDIA (NVDA) hit (LOW) $168 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for NVIDIA (NVDA) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the NVIDIA (NVDA) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.NVDA%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.NVDA%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will NVIDIA (NVDA) hit in May 2026?
events.description: What will NVIDIA (NVDA) hit in May 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: NVIDIA (NVDA) shares closed at $198.45 on May 1, reflecting trader consensus on sustained AI data center dominance despite recent rotation pressures, with market cap at $4.82 trillion and forward P/E of 24.45. Primary momentum stems from record Q4 fiscal 2026 revenue of $68 billion (up 73% year-over-year) and quarterly earnings growth of 94%, fueled by Blackwell GPU ramp-up and hyperscaler demand, though U.S. reviews of advanced chip exports to China introduce headwinds. Analysts project Q1 fiscal 2027 revenue at $78.8 billion and EPS of $1.77 for the May 20 earnings release— a pivotal catalyst that could propel shares toward the $269 average price target (high $380) or trigger volatility if guidance disappoints on competition or supply constraints.

markets.question: Will S&P 500 (SPX) hit $8,000 (HIGH) in June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point between market creation and market close on the final day of trading for June 2026, any 1-minute candle for S&P 500 (SPX) shows a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

All prices recorded during regular trading hours of the primary exchange for the instrument, as reflected in Yahoo Finance's 1-minute interval ("1m") data, will be considered.

Periods when the market is officially closed (e.g., holidays or maintenance breaks) will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Pakistan Meteorological Department’s recent heatwave alert for Sindh, pinpointing May 3 as the peak with maximum temperatures potentially soaring to 41°C in Karachi, underpins the 87.5% market-implied probability for 37°C or higher. This reflects observed highs of 36–38°C on May 1 and sustained hot, dry conditions on May 2, driven by a persistent high-pressure ridge promoting subsidence, clear skies, and intense solar heating amid low cloud cover. May climatology in coastal Karachi averages 35°C highs but routinely exceeds 37°C during pre-monsoon surges like this, with historical precedents over 40°C. While models show strong consensus, afternoon sea breezes introduce minor uncertainty; traders await final PMD bulletins and real-time observations resolving the market.

markets.question: Will Starmer say "Shadow Secretary" during the next Prime Minister's Questions event?
markets.description:
Keir Starmer is scheduled to participate in the Prime Minister's Questions (PMQs) in the Commons Chamber in the Palace of Westminster on every Wednesday. (https://www.parliament.uk/visiting/visiting-and-tours/watch-committees-and-debates/prime-ministers-questions/).

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Keir Starmer says the listed term during the next Prime Minister's Question Time event he participates in. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Any usage of the term regardless of context will count toward the resolution of this market.

Pluralization/possessive of the term will count toward the resolution of this market, however other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

If no such Prime Minister's Question Time event happens by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No". If Starmer ceases to be Prime Minister for any length of time by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

This market is explicitly about scheduled events featuring Keir Starmer titled "Prime Minister's Question Time" (https://www.parallelparliament.co.uk/mp/keir-starmer/dept/cabinet-office/calendar). Speeches, events, or comments that occur outside of named, scheduled events will not qualify toward this market's resolution. If the event contains a Q&A, it will count toward the resolution of this market.

The resolution source will be video of the events. Only remarks which are broadcast or streamed live will count toward this market's resolution.

The dominant Mandelson vetting scandal—revealing the peer's failed US security clearance—fuels demands for accountability, alongside foreign policy on Iran volatility and EU partnership pledges. Traders should monitor post-election dynamics and any snap parliamentary return, as these shape Starmer's responses on government delivery and scandals.

eventMetadata.context_description: Atlético Madrid, entrenched in fourth place in the La Liga table after 33 matches, host sixth-placed RC Celta de Vigo on May 9 at Riyadh Air Metropolitano in a pivotal late-season clash for Champions League qualification. Los Colchoneros' recent form includes gritty home wins over Athletic Bilbao (3-2) and Getafe (1-0), bolstered by strong head-to-head dominance (19 wins in 30 meetings), though injuries to José Giménez (muscle), Dávid Hancko (ankle), and Pablo Barrios (thigh) temper squad depth. Home advantage and table stakes shape trader consensus.

markets.question: Fed rate hike in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is increased at any point between January 1, 2026 and the Fed's December 2026 meeting, currently scheduled for December 8-9, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the Fed has released its rate change decision following its December meeting.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Federal Reserve (https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price an 81.5% implied probability against a Federal Reserve rate hike in 2026, reflecting the FOMC's April 29 decision to hold the federal funds rate steady at 3.50%-3.75% despite March CPI inflation accelerating to 3.3% year-over-year—its highest since May 2024. This hawkish dissent in an 8-4 vote underscores caution, yet the March dot plot still projects one rate cut this year amid a resilient labor market with unemployment dipping to 4.3% and 10-year Treasury yields hovering near 4.40%. Powell's remarks signaling tariff-driven price pressures will ease provide further reassurance, pricing out hikes unless upcoming April CPI (May 12 release) or June FOMC data surprises hotter.

markets.question: Jimmy Lai released by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Jimmy Lai is released from custody by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If Lai is released but remains under house arrest, the market will still resolve to "Yes".

If Lai is released on parole, bond, or any other condition that results in them leaving state custody, the market will resolve to "Yes".

Transporting Lai to another location of custody (e.g., a different prison, court, or hospital within the correctional system) will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Extradition of Lai to another country where he remains in custody will also not count.

Temporary outings from prison for purposes such as testifying in court, while still under the custody of correctional authorities, will not count as a release.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from relevant government authorities or corrections departments; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

These procedural finalities amid Beijing's firm control over Hong Kong judiciary underscore entrenched imprisonment, with no recent health reports or clemency signals. Barring unforeseen scenarios like a medical crisis warranting early release or high-level diplomatic pressure yielding pardon, the timeline leaves scant room for reversal.

James' absence from the April 30 Oakland County GOP debate—prioritizing congressional votes—sparked rival attacks labeling him an "empty suit" unable to "make it to Michigan," fueling perceptions of disengagement amid shouting matches on utility rates and income tax elimination. With the August 4 primary nearing, July televised debates and endorsements could tip the fragmented field.

markets.question: Will Analilia Mejia win the NJ-11 Special Election by 40% or more?
markets.description:
The special election for the seat for New Jersey's 11th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives is currently scheduled to take place on April 16, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the specified election.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first-place and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket.

If two or more candidates tie for the most valid votes in this election, and the tie is between two candidates listed, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the candidate whose listed last name comes first in alphabetical order. If the tie is between a listed candidate and an unlisted candidate, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed candidate. If the tie is between two or more unlisted candidates, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.nj.gov/state/.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors a 20-25% margin of victory for Democrat Analilia Mejia in New Jersey's 11th Congressional District special election, reflecting vote tallies with over 95% counted showing her leading Republican Joe Hathaway 60.2%-39.4%—a 21-point edge that has held steady since April 16. This commanding position stems from pre-election polls forecasting a double-digit lead in the Democratic-leaning suburban district, bolstered by strong progressive turnout after Mejia's February primary win and low special election participation favoring the incumbent party's base. While official certification by New Jersey election officials remains pending, scenarios like a disproportionate surge in uncounted provisional or absentee ballots for Hathaway could narrow the gap below 20%, though the stable count makes this unlikely.

markets.question: Counter-Strike: ASTRAL vs Bebop (BO3) - CCT Europe Closed Qualifier: Series #1 Play-In
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between ASTRAL and Bebop in the CCT Europe Closed Qualifier: Series #1 Play-In, initially scheduled for May 2 at 10:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "ASTRAL" if ASTRAL win the match against Bebop.

This market will resolve to "Bebop" if Bebop win the match against ASTRAL.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: ASTRAL and Bebop, both hovering around HLTV world rankings #101-104, meet in a high-stakes BO3 play-in for CCT Europe 2026 Series 1 Closed Qualifier advancement to the group stage. Map picks feature ASTRAL's Dust2 (57% win rate) first, Bebop's Inferno, with Nuke as decider—pistol rounds and first-kill advantages pivotal in this tier-3 matchup.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Los Angeles be between 58-59°F on May 2?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Los Angeles International Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 2 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Los Angeles International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/ca/los-angeles/KLAX.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket closely splits between 66-67°F (40.5%) and 68-69°F (42.0%) for the highest temperature at Los Angeles International Airport (KLAX) on May 2, reflecting National Weather Service (NWS) guidance forecasting a high near 69°F amid patchy morning fog and gradual clearing from a persistent marine layer. Cool Pacific sea surface temperatures around 60°F and light onshore southwest winds sustain a low-level temperature inversion, trapping stratiform clouds and limiting boundary layer mixing—key factors capping peaks below climatological May averages of 69°F. Ensemble model runs from GFS and ECMWF show convergence in the 67-70°F range, with uncertainty hinging on stratus dissipation timing; delayed clearing favors lower outcomes, while earlier burn-off supports 68-69°F. Real-time KLAX METARs will drive intraday shifts.

markets.question: Will Silver (SI) settle over $75 on the final trading day of June 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official CME settlement price for the Active Month of Silver futures on the final trading day of June 2026 is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Silver (SI) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (March, May, July, September, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month becomes a non-active month effective on its First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days during June on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Silver (SI) futures.

June 2026 SI futures hold near $76, embodying trader consensus for modest stability, supported by electronics and EV demand despite 19% photovoltaic silver usage cuts via industry thrifting. Critical ahead: May CPI on June 10, nonfarm payrolls early June, and FOMC June 16-17, where hotter inflation or soft labor could pressure rate cut odds, bolstering or weighing on silver via USD and Treasury yield dynamics.

eventMetadata.context_description: Cameron Young commands a five-stroke lead at 13-under par after bogey-free rounds of 64-67 through two rounds of the Cadillac Championship, a $20 million no-cut signature event at Trump National Doral's demanding Blue Monster course, solidifying top-20 positioning for favorites in the 72-man field with 37 top-50 OWGR players. Jordan Spieth, Nick Taylor, and Alex Smalley sit tied for second at 8-under, while world No. 1 Scottie Scheffler lurks at 6-under tied seventh after a steadier second round. Jake Knapp's pre-tournament withdrawal due to a thumb sprain opened the door for Kristoffer Reitan, now also at 6-under; gusty winds expected for Saturday's third round and Sunday finale could introduce volatility on the renovated layout's strategic hazards.

markets.question: Will Pixelworks (PXLW) beat quarterly earnings?
markets.description:
As of market creation, Pixelworks is estimated to release earnings on May 12, 2026. The Street consensus estimate for Pixelworks’s non-GAAP EPS for the relevant quarter is $-0.14 as of market creation. This market will resolve to "Yes" if Pixelworks reports non-GAAP EPS greater than $-0.14 for the relevant quarter in its next quarterly earnings release. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." The resolution source will be the non-GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents. 

If Pixelworks releases earnings without non-GAAP EPS, then the market will resolve according to the non-GAAP EPS figure reported by SeekingAlpha. If no such figure is published within 96h of market close (4:00:00pm ET) on the day earnings are announced, the market will resolve according to the GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents; or, if not published there, according to the GAAP EPS provided by SeekingAlpha. If no GAAP EPS number is available from either source at that time, the market will resolve to “No.” (For the purposes of this market, GAAP EPS refers to diluted GAAP EPS, unless it is not published, in which case it refers to basic GAAP EPS.)

If the company does not release earnings within 45 calendar days of the estimated earnings date, this market will resolve to “No.” 

Note: Subsequent restatements, corrections, or revisions made to the initially announced non-GAAP EPS figure will not qualify for resolution, except in the case of obvious and immediate mistakes (e.g., fat finger errors, as with Lyft's (LYFT) earnings release in February 2024).
Note: The strike prices used in these markets are derived from SeekingAlpha estimates, and reflect the consensus of sell-side analyst estimates for non-GAAP EPS.
Note: All figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.
Note: For the purposes of this market, IFRS EPS will be treated as GAAP EPS.
Note: If multiple versions of non-GAAP EPS are published, the market will resolve according to the primary headline non-GAAP EPS number, which is typically presented on a diluted basis. If diluted is not published, then basic non-GAAP EPS will qualify.
Note: All figures are expressed in USD, unless otherwise indicated.
Note: For primarily internationally listed companies, this market refers specifically to the shares traded in the United States on U.S. stock exchanges such as the NYSE or Nasdaq. In cases where the company trades in the U.S. through an American Depositary Receipt (ADR) or American Depositary Share (ADS), this market will refer to the ADR/ADS.

markets.question: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
events.description:

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a 76% implied probability against the U.S. enacting a comprehensive AI safety bill before 2027, driven by congressional gridlock and the Trump administration's March 20 National AI Legislative Framework prioritizing light-touch regulations, innovation, and federal preemption of state laws over stringent safety mandates. Despite bipartisan introductions like the AI Guardrails Act and bills targeting deepfakes or children's AI protections advancing in Senate committees in late April, no broad legislation has cleared key hurdles amid partisan divides on regulatory scope. States continue filling the void with 25 new AI laws by early April, underscoring federal inaction, while upcoming midterms and appropriations deadlines offer slim windows for passage.

markets.question: Will Xi Jinping win the Nobel Peace Prize in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize, as announced by the Norwegian Nobel Committee.

If multiple listed individuals or entities jointly receive the prize, the market will resolve to a single winner. If Donald Trump, Volodymyr Zelenskyy,  Benjamin Netanyahu, Vladimir Putin, or Elon Musk are among the recipients, the market will resolve to the highest-ranked among them in that exact order of precedence. If none of those five are among the winners and the prize is awarded jointly to at least one listed individual and at least one listed organization, the market will resolve in favor of the individual. If no prioritized individuals are among the winners and all listed recipients are of the same type (all individuals or all organizations), the market will resolve to the person whose last name, or the entity’s name, comes first in alphabetical order as listed in this market.

If no official announcement regarding the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize has been made by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other."

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement from the Norwegian Nobel Committee.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a fragmented field for the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize following the Norwegian Nobel Committee's April 30 announcement of 287 nominees—208 individuals and 79 organizations—sparking early speculation without a dominant frontrunner. Yulia Navalnaya's slight edge at 7.5% implied probability stems from her continued advocacy against Russian authoritarianism as Alexei Navalny's widow, amplified by media buzz; Volodymyr Zelenskyy (6.8%) benefits from symbolic wartime leadership in Ukraine, recently nominated by an Oslo professor alongside its people; and Donald Trump (6.5%) gains from reported endorsements by Pakistan, Cambodia, and Israel leaders amid diplomatic claims. Pope Leo XIV (4.0%) rides grassroots momentum from his anti-war pronouncements and meetings with figures like Maria Corina Machado. With odds below 10% across the board, uncertainty persists due to the secretive selection process and evolving geopolitics, with the October 9 announcement as the key catalyst.

markets.question: Manchester United FC vs. Liverpool FC: O/U 4.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Premier League game between Manchester United FC and Liverpool FC, scheduled for May 3 at 10:30 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Manchester United FC and Liverpool FC combine to score 5 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 5, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on premierleague.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Manchester United host Liverpool in a pivotal Premier League Matchweek 35 clash at Old Trafford on May 3, with United sitting third in the table and Liverpool fifth amid a tight race for Champions League spots. Defensive woes dominate trader sentiment: United face suspensions for Harry Maguire and Lisandro Martínez, plus injuries to Matthijs de Ligt, Leny Yoro, and Patrick Dorgu, thinning their backline after a recent red card and international break knocks. Liverpool's crisis deepened yesterday when backup keeper Giorgi Mamardashvili suffered a serious injury on a stretcher during match action—joining long-term absentee Alisson—while Hugo Ekitike (ankle) and Jeremie Frimpong (three weeks out) remain sidelined. United won the reverse fixture 2-1 at Anfield in October, but both sides' recent form and injury hits heighten upset potential in this fierce Northwest Derby.

markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team is determined as the champion of the WNBA for the 2026 season. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed participant to named as the champion of WNBA for the 2026 season per the rules of WNBA (e.g., participant is eliminated), the corresponding market will resolve to "No".

If multiple teams are declared winners, this market will resolve in favor of the team whose listed name comes first alphabetically.

If this event is cancelled, postponed after November 14, 11:59 PM ET, or a champion has not been declared within this timeframe, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source will be official information from the WNBA: however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: New York Liberty top trader consensus at 35% implied probability for the 2026 WNBA championship, bolstered by offseason addition of three-time All-Star Satou Sabally to their elite core of Breanna Stewart (three-time champion), Sabrina Ionescu, and Jonquel Jones, enhancing title contention despite a recent preseason loss to Indiana. Fever's 21% reflects rising optimism for a healthy Caitlin Clark, Aliyah Boston, and Kelsey Mitchell trio after strong 2025 momentum and free agency reinforcements. Golden State Valkyries at 18.5% ride inaugural playoff success and protected assets through the April 3 expansion draft for Portland Fire and Toronto Tempo. Los Angeles Sparks (15.6%) and defending champion Las Vegas Aces (15.5%, fresh off re-signing A'ja Wilson) benefit from roster reloads amid league expansion dilution, with preseason tune-ups underscoring competitive depth ahead of tip-off next week.

markets.question: Will Robinhood Markets, Inc. (HOOD) hit (HIGH) $90 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Robinhood Markets, Inc. (HOOD) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Robinhood Markets, Inc. (HOOD) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.HOOD%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.HOOD%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Robinhood Markets, Inc. (HOOD) hit in May 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Robinhood Markets (HOOD) shares closed at $73.66 on May 1, 2026, down over 12% from late-April highs near $85 following the April 28 Q1 earnings release, which reported total net revenues of $1.07 billion—up 15% year-over-year but missing consensus estimates of $1.14 billion—and diluted EPS of $0.38 versus $0.39 expected. Robust net deposits of $18 billion, reflecting 22% annualized growth, alongside record transaction activity in prediction markets (up 320% year-over-year) and index options, underscored user engagement amid fintech expansion into derivatives. Analyst price targets average around $100, with upside tied to trading volumes and crypto exposure; traders eye monthly metrics releases and broader market risk appetite through May resolution.

markets.question: Arizona Diamondbacks vs. Chicago Cubs: O/U 7.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming MLB game between the Arizona Diamondbacks and Chicago Cubs, scheduled for May 2 at 2:20 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if the Arizona Diamondbacks and Chicago Cubs combine to score 8 or more runs in this game.

If the combined total is less than 8, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

To know when a postponed game will be played, please check the home team's schedule on MLB.com for the listed team and look for the game described as a makeup game.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official final statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Arizona Diamondbacks vs. Chicago Cubs
events.description:

This market will resolve to "Chicago Cubs" if the Chicago Cubs win the game.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, or ends in a tie, this market will resolve 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official final statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Chicago Cubs hold trader consensus edge over Arizona Diamondbacks in this Wrigley Field matchup, driven by a stark pitching mismatch with Shota Imanaga (2-2, 3.15 ERA) facing struggling Ryne Nelson (1-2, 7.71 ERA) after Chicago's 6-5 series-opening victory yesterday. Cubs (20-12, NL Central leaders) boast home-field advantage and a nine-game Wrigley win streak, though second baseman Nico Hoerner sits with left-sided neck tightness, shifting Shaw to 2B. Diamondbacks (16-15) welcome catcher Gabriel Moreno back from oblique IL but must overcome road fatigue and bullpen strain post-close loss, where Ildemaro Vargas notched four hits. Cool 50°F temps and potential winds favor pitchers, tempering run totals.

markets.question: Will Charles Leclerc get pole position at the 2026 F1 Miami Grand Prix?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket on the driver who achieves pole position at the 2026 F1 Miami Grand Prix, scheduled for May 1, 2026.

If the 2026 F1 Miami Grand Prix is canceled or rescheduled to a date after May 8, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve in favor of the driver who is officially recognized by Formula 1 as having set the fastest time during the qualifying session for the 2026 F1 Miami Grand Prix. The market will be settled based on the FIA's official qualifying results, regardless of any subsequent penalties, disqualifications, or changes to the starting grid.

For example, if a driver sets the fastest qualifying time but later receives a grid penalty or is moved down the starting order, the market will still resolve to “Yes” for that driver.

The resolution source will be the official Formula 1 website and a consensus of credible sports news reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Lando Norris' commanding sprint qualifying pole position, edging Kimi Antonelli by just 0.222 seconds ahead of Oscar Piastri (+0.230s), has solidified McLaren and Mercedes as the pole favorites for the Miami Grand Prix main qualifying, with trader consensus pricing Norris at 50% implied probability and Antonelli close at 42.5%. Despite Ferrari's Charles Leclerc topping Friday's extended FP1 ahead of Max Verstappen, Norris' purple sector one-lap pace and Antonelli's recovery from power unit issues underscore the razor-thin margins in a bunched top field. Liam Lawson's 26% reflects RB's potential upside, but the wisdom of crowds emphasizes McLaren's qualifying edge on Miami's wall-lined street circuit, where clean laps and tire prep will decide Q3 supremacy.

eventMetadata.context_description: Progressive challenger Oliver Adams Larkin leads trader consensus at 37.5% implied probability over incumbent Rep. Jared Moskowitz (20%) for Florida's 23rd Congressional District Democratic primary on August 18, reflecting momentum from DSA's first federal endorsement of 2026 (April) and the Progressive Change Campaign Committee's backing, amid Larkin's attacks on Moskowitz's AIPAC ties and Gaza policy votes. Late April redistricting proposals by Gov. DeSantis reshaped FL-23, removing Moskowitz's Boca Raton home turf and incorporating bluer West Palm Beach areas, potentially boosting Larkin's progressive appeal in the closed primary. Though Moskowitz holds a Q1 fundraising edge and led a March poll 45%-11%, recent voter biography tests show Larkin ahead 51%, highlighting uncertainty and base turnout dynamics.

markets.question: Will SpaceX's market cap be between $1.8T and $2.0T at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on SpaceX's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no SpaceX IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before 2028".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on SpaceX’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket strongly favors SpaceX achieving a closing IPO market cap exceeding $2 trillion (54.5% implied probability), propelled by the company's April 1, 2026, confidential SEC filing targeting a June listing with a $75 billion raise at over $2 trillion valuation—far surpassing its December 2025 tender offer at $800 billion and recent secondary sales near $1.5 trillion. Starlink's surging satellite internet revenues, funding $20 billion-plus annual capex for constellation expansion, combined with Starship Version 3 milestones—including recent engine tests and the impending Flight 12 test—bolster optimism for reusable launch dominance and Mars ambitions. Upcoming early June roadshow and dual-class structure ensuring Elon Musk's control heighten anticipation, though Starship orbital refueling risks and regulatory scrutiny could temper outcomes.

markets.question: Will Travis Kelce retire before next season?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Travis Kelce retires from the NFL before the start of the 2026-2027 NFL regular season. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An official announcement from Kelce that he is retiring will qualify even if his official retirement has not yet occurred, as long as the announced retirement is stated to take effect before the start of the 2026-2027 NFL regular season. Announcements that Kelce will retire after the start of the 2026-2027 NFL regular season will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from Travis Kelce, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Travis Kelce's recent one-year contract extension with the Kansas City Chiefs, signed in March 2026 after dispelling offseason retirement rumors following a playoff-missing 2025 campaign, anchors trader consensus at 96.4% implied probability against retirement before the 2026 NFL season. Chiefs GM Brett Veach confirmed on May 1 that Kelce committed to returning immediately post-season finale, emphasizing his refusal to "go out like this" amid motivation for another run with QB Patrick Mahomes. At 36, the future Hall of Fame tight end remains productive without major injury concerns on official reports. Barring an unforeseen health issue, personal life shift, or abrupt announcement before training camp, market resolution favors continuation into his 14th season.

eventMetadata.context_description: Bayern Munich's mounting injury concerns, including Serge Gnabry's long-term adductor tear announced April 18 and Lennart Karl's hamstring strain earlier this month, have thinned their attacking options ahead of the May 2 Bundesliga clash at Allianz Arena against relegation-haunted Heidenheim, who sit bottom of the table after a 1-2 loss to Freiburg on April 19. Leading the standings with 76 points and a +78 goal difference from 29 games, Bayern crushed Heidenheim 4-0 away in December but now face squad depth issues that heighten uncertainty around the exact scoreline. Heidenheim's poor away form and desperation for points keep defensive setups tight, contributing to trader consensus bunching probabilities evenly across outcomes as the wisdom of crowds reflects caution on a precise prediction in this mismatched but unpredictable matchup.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NV-01 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NV-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).
